votes.reason: The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification, including primality testing, digit sum calculation, and finding the maximum prime in a list.
The Python code correctly implements the Coq specifications, including the primality test, base-10 digit sum, and finding the maximum prime in a list.
The Python code correctly implements the Coq specifications, including primality testing, digit summation, and finding the maximum prime in a list.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification for concrete inputs. sum_list computes the sum of a list. digits_value correctly computes the value with least-significant digit first (matching Coq's recursive definition). prime_Z correctly checks primality. sum_digits_10 checks if s equals the sum of decimal digits of n (for n >= 0). max_prime_in_list checks if p is the maximum prime in the list. skjkasdkd_spec finds the maximum prime in lst and checks if res equals the sum of its digits. The key functions all produce the same concrete results as the Coq definitions.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification for concrete inputs. sum_list computes the sum of a list. digits_value correctly computes the value with least-significant digit first. digit and all_digits check digit validity. divides_Z correctly checks divisibility. prime_Z correctly checks primality (the Coq definition is equivalent to standard primality). sum_digits_10 checks if s equals the sum of decimal digits of n (for n >= 0). max_prime_in_list checks if p is the maximum prime in the list. skjkasdkd_spec finds the maximum prime in lst and checks if res equals its digit sum. The Python implementations match the Coq specifications on all concrete inputs.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification for concrete inputs. sum_list computes the sum of a list. digits_value correctly computes the value with least-significant digit first. digit and all_digits check digit validity. divides_Z correctly checks divisibility. prime_Z correctly checks primality (the Coq definition requires no divisors from 2 to n-1, which is equivalent to the Python trial division). sum_digits_10 checks if s equals the sum of decimal digits of n (for n >= 0). max_prime_in_list checks if p is the maximum prime in the list. skjkasdkd_spec finds the maximum prime and checks if res equals its digit sum. All functions produce the same concrete results as the Coq specification.

Both select the maximum prime in the list and check that res equals its decimal digit sum; helper functions (sum, digits_value, digit checks, divisibility, primality) align with the Coq definitions for all concrete inputs.

Both compute res as the sum of base-10 digits of the maximum prime in the list (if any). Helper functions (digits, digits_value, divides, primality, and sum_digits_10 for n>=0) align with the Coq definitions on all integers. Negative numbers are not prime in both, and membership/max logic matches the Coq max_prime_in_list predicate.

Both compute the sum of decimal digits of the maximum prime in the list for all concrete inputs. Primality is checked for n>=2, negatives are excluded, and the digit-sum matches the existential Coq definition. Edge cases (no primes, n=0) are handled consistently.
